content.claim: European Court of Human Rights ruled in Szolcsán v. Hungary (2023) that Roma segregation in schools breaches discrimination prohibition; EU infringement proceedings ongoing since 2016; catchment-based school network decisions are subject to non-discrimination legal constraints.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Ruling concerns segregation by ethnicity, not school size or rural depopulation per se
Does not specify which school configuration (closure vs. retention) best satisfies non-discrimination obligation
Legal constraint is binding but leaves scope ambiguity for policy design
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
